3.why: We need the size to wire up next pointers and visited flags.
3.invariant: n is the number of nodes.

4.why: nxt[i] will hold the id node i points to.

5.happening: Loop over every node to set its next pointer.
5.why: Each node needs to know its successor.
5.invariant: Nodes below i are already wired.

6.happening: Node {i} is not the tail — its next is the following node.
6.why: Interior nodes point at the next index.
6.invariant: Only the tail can start a cycle.

6.happening: Node {i} is the tail — its next is the cycle target.
6.why: The tail is where a cycle (if any) links back.
6.invariant: The tail's next is pos, or -1.

7.why: Interior nodes link to their immediate successor.

9.why: pos is the cycle target; -1 means the list simply ends.
9.invariant: A back-edge to pos creates the cycle.

10.why: This is the extra O(n) memory that records which nodes we've seen.
10.invariant: visited[i] tells whether node i was reached before.

11.happening: Loop over every node to initialize its flag.
11.invariant: Flags below i are already set.

13.why: We walk the list starting from the head.
13.invariant: cur is the node we're currently standing on; -1 means none.

14.invariant: found becomes True only on a revisit.

15.happening: cur points at a real node — keep walking.
15.why: We continue until we either revisit a node or fall off the end.
15.invariant: Every node before cur has been marked visited.

15.happening: cur is -1 — we walked off the end with no revisit.
15.why: Reaching null proves the list terminates.
15.invariant: found is still False — no cycle.

16.happening: Node {cur} was already visited — we've looped back!
16.why: Reaching a node twice can only happen inside a cycle.
16.invariant: A revisit proves a cycle exists.

16.happening: Node {cur} is new — we haven't seen it before.
16.why: A first visit doesn't prove anything yet; keep walking.
16.invariant: cur has not been marked visited yet.

17.happening: Record that a cycle was found.
17.why: We stepped onto an already-visited node.

18.happening: Stop early — the answer is settled.
18.why: Once a revisit is seen there's nothing left to check.
18.invariant: The loop ends with found = True.

19.why: So that stepping onto it again later is detected as a cycle.
19.invariant: Every node walked so far is marked visited.

20.happening: Follow the next pointer from node {cur}.
20.why: We advance to the successor node (or -1 at the end).
20.invariant: cur becomes the next node in the chain.

21.happening: Return found — whether the list has a cycle.

init.happening: Building the linked list (with any cycle link), clearing visited flags, and placing cur at the head.
init.why: We need the chain and empty flags before the walk.
init.invariant: cur starts at the head; no node is visited yet.

loop.happening: Checking whether cur still points at a real node.
loop.why: The walk continues until a revisit or the end.
loop.invariant: Every node before cur is marked visited.

check.happening: Testing whether the current node was already visited.
check.why: A repeat visit is the signature of a cycle.

update.happening: Recording a revisit, or marking the current node visited.
update.why: The visited trail is how a future repeat is detected.

move.happening: Following the next pointer to the next node.
move.why: cur advances one node at a time along the chain.
move.invariant: cur is the next node (or -1 at the end).

return.happening: Returning whether a cycle was found.
return.why: Either we revisited a node or reached the end.
